name: terraform
description: Infrastructure as Code tool for building, changing, and versioning cloud infrastructure

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Initialize a Terraform working directory by downloading providers, modules, and configuring the backend

options.name: -backend
options.description: Configure the backend for this configuration (default true)

options.name: -backend-config
options.description: Backend configuration key=value or path to a backend config file

options.name: -from-module
options.description: Copy the contents of the given module into the target directory before init

options.name: -get
options.description: Download modules for this configuration (default true)

options.name: -input
options.description: Ask for input for variables if not directly set (default true)

options.name: -lock
options.description: Don't hold a state lock during init (default true)

options.name: -lock-timeout
options.description: Duration to retry a state lock (default 0s)

options.name: -no-color
options.description: Disable color codes in output

options.name: -plugin-dir
options.description: Directory containing plugin binaries; disables automatic plugin download

options.name: -reconfigure
options.description: Reconfigure the backend, ignoring any saved configuration

options.name: -migrate-state
options.description: Migrate existing state to the new backend configuration

options.name: -upgrade
options.description: Upgrade modules and plugins to the latest allowed versions

options.name: -lockfile
options.description: Set a dependency lockfile mode (readonly)

options.name: -ignore-remote-version
options.description: Ignore checking that the remote and local Terraform versions are compatible

subcommands.name: plan
subcommands.description: Create an execution plan showing what actions Terraform will take to reach the desired state

options.name: -destroy
options.description: Generate a plan to destroy all resources

options.name: -refresh-only
options.description: Generate a plan to update state to match remote objects

options.name: -refresh
options.description: Skip checking for external changes during planning (default true)

options.name: -replace
options.description: Force replacement of a particular resource instance (addr)

options.name: -target
options.description: Limit the planning scope to specific resources

options.name: -var
options.description: Set a variable value (key=value)

options.name: -var-file
options.description: Set variables from a file

options.name: -out
options.description: Write the plan to a file

options.name: -input
options.description: Ask for input for variables if not directly set (default true)

options.name: -lock
options.description: Don't hold a state lock during operations (default true)

options.name: -lock-timeout
options.description: Duration to retry a state lock (default 0s)

options.name: -no-color
options.description: Disable color codes in output

options.name: -parallelism
options.description: Limit the number of concurrent operations (default 10)

options.name: -compact-warnings
options.description: Show warnings in a more compact form

options.name: -detailed-exitcode
options.description: Return detailed exit codes: 0=no changes, 1=error, 2=changes present

options.name: -generate-config-out
options.description: Generate configuration for import blocks and write to file

subcommands.name: apply
subcommands.description: Apply the changes required to reach the desired state of the configuration

args.name: <plan-file>
args.description: Path to a previously saved plan file

options.name: -auto-approve
options.description: Skip interactive approval of the plan

options.name: -refresh-only
options.description: Apply only state refresh changes

options.name: -refresh
options.description: Skip checking for external changes (default true)

options.name: -replace
options.description: Force replacement of a particular resource instance

options.name: -target
options.description: Limit the apply scope to specific resources

options.name: -var
options.description: Set a variable value (key=value)

options.name: -var-file
options.description: Set variables from a file

options.name: -input
options.description: Ask for input for variables if not directly set (default true)

options.name: -lock
options.description: Don't hold a state lock during operations (default true)

options.name: -lock-timeout
options.description: Duration to retry a state lock (default 0s)

options.name: -no-color
options.description: Disable color codes in output

options.name: -parallelism
options.description: Limit the number of concurrent operations (default 10)

options.name: -compact-warnings
options.description: Show warnings in a more compact form

options.name: -destroy
options.description: Alias for terraform destroy

subcommands.name: destroy
subcommands.description: Destroy all remote objects managed by the current Terraform configuration

options.name: -auto-approve
options.description: Skip interactive approval before destroying

options.name: -target
options.description: Limit the destroy scope to specific resources

options.name: -var
options.description: Set a variable value (key=value)

options.name: -var-file
options.description: Set variables from a file

options.name: -input
options.description: Ask for input for variables if not directly set (default true)

options.name: -lock
options.description: Don't hold a state lock during operations (default true)

options.name: -lock-timeout
options.description: Duration to retry a state lock (default 0s)

options.name: -no-color
options.description: Disable color codes in output

options.name: -parallelism
options.description: Limit the number of concurrent operations (default 10)

options.name: -compact-warnings
options.description: Show warnings in a more compact form

options.name: -refresh
options.description: Skip checking for external changes (default true)

subcommands.name: validate
subcommands.description: Validate the configuration files in a directory for correctness and internal consistency

options.name: -json
options.description: Produce output in machine-readable JSON format

options.name: -no-color
options.description: Disable color codes in output

options.name: -no-tests
options.description: Skip validation of test files

subcommands.name: fmt
subcommands.description: Rewrite Terraform configuration files to a canonical format and style

args.name: <target>
args.description: File or directory to format (default: current directory)

options.name: -list
options.description: List files whose formatting differs (default true)

options.name: -write
options.description: Write formatted output back to source files (default true)

options.name: -diff
options.description: Display diffs of formatting changes

options.name: -check
options.description: Check if input is formatted; return exit status 0 if formatted, non-zero otherwise

options.name: -recursive
options.description: Also process files in subdirectories

options.name: -no-color
options.description: Disable color codes in output

subcommands.name: show
subcommands.description: Show the current state or a saved plan in a human-readable form

args.name: <path>
args.description: Path to a state file or plan file

options.name: -json
options.description: Produce output in machine-readable JSON format

options.name: -no-color
options.description: Disable color codes in output

subcommands.name: output
subcommands.description: Read an output variable from the Terraform state file

args.name: <name>
args.description: Name of the output to read

options.name: -json
options.description: Produce output in machine-readable JSON format

options.name: -raw
options.description: Print the raw string value of the output without quotes

options.name: -no-color
options.description: Disable color codes in output

options.name: -state
options.description: Path to the state file to read (default: terraform.tfstate)

subcommands.name: refresh
subcommands.description: Update the state file to match real-world infrastructure (deprecated, prefer plan -refresh-only)

options.name: -target
options.description: Limit refresh to specific resources

options.name: -var
options.description: Set a variable value (key=value)

options.name: -var-file
options.description: Set variables from a file

options.name: -input
options.description: Ask for input for variables if not directly set (default true)

options.name: -lock
options.description: Don't hold a state lock during operations (default true)

options.name: -lock-timeout
options.description: Duration to retry a state lock (default 0s)

options.name: -no-color
options.description: Disable color codes in output

options.name: -parallelism
options.description: Limit the number of concurrent operations (default 10)

subcommands.name: import
subcommands.description: Import existing infrastructure into your Terraform state

args.name: <addr>
args.description: Terraform resource address (e.g. aws_instance.web)

args.name: <id>
args.description: Provider-specific resource ID

options.name: -config
options.description: Path to Terraform configuration directory (default: current dir)

options.name: -input
options.description: Ask for input for variables if not directly set (default true)

options.name: -lock
options.description: Don't hold a state lock during import (default true)

options.name: -lock-timeout
options.description: Duration to retry a state lock (default 0s)

options.name: -no-color
options.description: Disable color codes in output

options.name: -var
options.description: Set a variable value (key=value)

options.name: -var-file
options.description: Set variables from a file

options.name: -ignore-remote-version
options.description: Ignore remote version check for state

options.name: -allow-missing-config
options.description: Allow import when no resource configuration block exists

subcommands.name: taint
subcommands.description: Mark a resource instance as tainted, forcing it to be destroyed and recreated on the next apply (deprecated, prefer -replace)

args.name: <addr>
args.description: Address of the resource to taint

options.name: -allow-missing
options.description: Succeed even if the resource is missing

options.name: -lock
options.description: Don't hold a state lock during operations (default true)

options.name: -lock-timeout
options.description: Duration to retry a state lock (default 0s)

options.name: -no-color
options.description: Disable color codes in output

options.name: -ignore-remote-version
options.description: Ignore remote version check for state

subcommands.name: untaint
subcommands.description: Remove the taint from a resource instance, allowing it to persist on the next apply

args.name: <addr>
args.description: Address of the resource to untaint

options.name: -allow-missing
options.description: Succeed even if the resource is missing

options.name: -lock
options.description: Don't hold a state lock during operations (default true)

options.name: -lock-timeout
options.description: Duration to retry a state lock (default 0s)

options.name: -no-color
options.description: Disable color codes in output

options.name: -ignore-remote-version
options.description: Ignore remote version check for state

subcommands.name: state list
subcommands.description: List resources within the Terraform state

args.name: <filter>
args.description: Address filter pattern

options.name: -state
options.description: Path to a state file (default: terraform.tfstate)

options.name: -id
options.description: Filter by resource ID

subcommands.name: state show
subcommands.description: Show the attributes of a single resource in the state

args.name: <addr>
args.description: Address of the resource to show

options.name: -state
options.description: Path to a state file (default: terraform.tfstate)

subcommands.name: state mv
subcommands.description: Move a resource to a different address in the state

args.name: <source>
args.description: Current address of the resource

args.name: <destination>
args.description: New address for the resource

options.name: -dry-run
options.description: Preview the move without actually performing it

options.name: -lock
options.description: Don't hold a state lock during operations (default true)

options.name: -lock-timeout
options.description: Duration to retry a state lock (default 0s)

options.name: -state
options.description: Path to the source state file

options.name: -state-out
options.description: Path to the destination state file

options.name: -ignore-remote-version
options.description: Ignore remote version check for state

subcommands.name: state rm
subcommands.description: Remove resources from the Terraform state without destroying them

args.name: <addr>
args.description: Address of the resource(s) to remove

options.name: -dry-run
options.description: Preview the removal without actually performing it

options.name: -lock
options.description: Don't hold a state lock during operations (default true)

options.name: -lock-timeout
options.description: Duration to retry a state lock (default 0s)

options.name: -state
options.description: Path to the state file

options.name: -ignore-remote-version
options.description: Ignore remote version check for state

subcommands.name: state pull
subcommands.description: Pull the current state and output it to stdout

subcommands.name: state push
subcommands.description: Update remote state from a local state file

args.name: <path>
args.description: Path to the state file to push

options.name: -force
options.description: Write the state even if lineages don't match or serial is lower

options.name: -lock
options.description: Don't hold a state lock during operations (default true)

options.name: -lock-timeout
options.description: Duration to retry a state lock (default 0s)

subcommands.name: state replace-provider
subcommands.description: Replace provider in the state

args.name: <from>
args.description: Current provider address (e.g. registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/aws)

args.name: <to>
args.description: New provider address

options.name: -auto-approve
options.description: Skip interactive approval

options.name: -lock
options.description: Don't hold a state lock during operations (default true)

options.name: -lock-timeout
options.description: Duration to retry a state lock (default 0s)

options.name: -ignore-remote-version
options.description: Ignore remote version check for state

subcommands.name: workspace new
subcommands.description: Create a new Terraform workspace

args.name: <name>
args.description: Name of the new workspace

options.name: -lock
options.description: Don't hold a state lock (default true)

options.name: -lock-timeout
options.description: Duration to retry a state lock (default 0s)

options.name: -state
options.description: Copy an existing state file into the new workspace

subcommands.name: workspace select
subcommands.description: Switch to an existing Terraform workspace

args.name: <name>
args.description: Name of the workspace to select

options.name: -or-create
options.description: Create the workspace if it doesn't exist

subcommands.name: workspace list
subcommands.description: List all Terraform workspaces

subcommands.name: workspace show
subcommands.description: Show the name of the current Terraform workspace

subcommands.name: workspace delete
subcommands.description: Delete an existing Terraform workspace

args.name: <name>
args.description: Name of the workspace to delete

options.name: -force
options.description: Remove a workspace even if it has existing resources

options.name: -lock
options.description: Don't hold a state lock (default true)

options.name: -lock-timeout
options.description: Duration to retry a state lock (default 0s)

subcommands.name: providers
subcommands.description: Show the providers required by the current configuration

subcommands.name: providers mirror
subcommands.description: Download the providers required by the current configuration into a local directory

args.name: <target-dir>
args.description: Directory to store the mirrored providers

options.name: -platform
options.description: Target platform(s) to mirror (os_arch format)

subcommands.name: providers lock
subcommands.description: Write the dependency lock file for providers

options.name: -platform
options.description: Target platform(s) to include in the lock file (os_arch format)

options.name: -fs-mirror
options.description: Filesystem mirror directory to use

options.name: -net-mirror
options.description: Network mirror URL to use

subcommands.name: providers schema
subcommands.description: Print the schemas for the providers used in the configuration

options.name: -json
options.description: Output the schemas in JSON format

subcommands.name: graph
subcommands.description: Generate a visual representation of the dependency graph in DOT format

options.name: -type
options.description: Type of graph to output (plan, plan-refresh-only, plan-destroy, apply)

options.name: -draw-cycles
options.description: Highlight any cycles in the graph with colored edges

options.name: -plan
options.description: Render the graph based on a specific plan file

subcommands.name: console
subcommands.description: Open an interactive console for evaluating Terraform expressions

options.name: -state
options.description: Path to a state file to use for evaluation

options.name: -var
options.description: Set a variable value (key=value)

options.name: -var-file
options.description: Set variables from a file

options.name: -plan
options.description: Use a plan file for evaluation context

subcommands.name: force-unlock
subcommands.description: Manually unlock the state for the current configuration

args.name: <lock-id>
args.description: Lock ID from the lock error message

options.name: -force
options.description: Don't ask for input for unlock confirmation

subcommands.name: login
subcommands.description: Obtain and save credentials for a remote host (Terraform Cloud by default)

args.name: <hostname>
args.description: Hostname of the Terraform service (default: app.terraform.io)

subcommands.name: logout
subcommands.description: Remove locally-stored credentials for a remote host

args.name: <hostname>
args.description: Hostname of the Terraform service (default: app.terraform.io)

subcommands.name: version
subcommands.description: Print the Terraform version and installed provider versions

options.name: -json
options.description: Output version information in JSON format

subcommands.name: get
subcommands.description: Download and update modules mentioned in the root module

options.name: -update
options.description: Check all modules for updates and download updates if needed

options.name: -no-color
options.description: Disable color codes in output

subcommands.name: test
subcommands.description: Execute integration tests for Terraform modules

options.name: -cloud-run
options.description: Run tests on Terraform Cloud in the specified organization

options.name: -filter
options.description: Only run test files that match the given filter glob pattern

options.name: -json
options.description: Produce output in machine-readable JSON format

options.name: -no-color
options.description: Disable color codes in output

options.name: -test-directory
options.description: Set the test directory (default: tests)

options.name: -var
options.description: Set a variable value (key=value)

options.name: -var-file
options.description: Set variables from a file

options.name: -verbose
options.description: Print the plan or state for each run block as it executes

globalOptions.name: -chdir
globalOptions.description: Switch to a different working directory before executing the command

detectors.name: state_resources
detectors.description: All resources tracked in the current Terraform state

detectors.name: outputs
detectors.description: All outputs from the current Terraform state in JSON format

detectors.name: providers
detectors.description: Providers required by the current configuration

detectors.name: tf_version
detectors.description: Terraform version and installed provider versions

detectors.name: plan_summary
detectors.description: Quick summary of pending infrastructure changes

detectors.name: tf_files
detectors.description: Terraform configuration files in the current directory

detectors.name: variables
detectors.description: Variable names defined in Terraform configuration files

detectors.name: modules
detectors.description: Module sources referenced in the configuration

detectors.name: backend_config
detectors.description: Backend configuration block from Terraform files
